title: MAGA Is Starting to Look Beyond Trump | WIRED
description: A seemingly endless torrent of criticism directed toward Trump from former MAGA allies suggests the president is losing support from his base.
author: DAVID GILBERT

title: A Hot-Air Balloon Landed in a California Backyard. The Owner Says It's a 'Very Rare' Event | WIRED
description: The CEO of Magical Adventures Balloon Rides tells WIRED how the pilot made a safe landing after they got stranded over a neighborhood.
author: BRIAN BARRETT

title: There’s New Evidence for How Loneliness Affects Memory in Old Age | WIRED
description: A longitudinal study found that loneliness is more closely linked to lapses in immediate and delayed recall than to the overall speed of cognitive decline.
author: JORGE GARAY

title: OpenAI Executive Kevin Weil Is Leaving the Company | WIRED
description: The former Instagram VP is departing the ChatGPT-maker, which is folding the AI science application he led into Codex.
author: MAXWELL ZEFF

title: MAGA Indians Went All In on Trump. Many Right-Wingers Can’t Stand Them | WIRED
description: South Asians are a powerful, visible minority in the Trump administration. They’re also facing a racist backlash, fueled in part by the white nationalist Groyper movement.
author: ASHWIN RODRIGUES

title: What a Bloody San Francisco Street Brawl Tells Us About the Age of Citizen Surveillance | WIRED
description: When a homeless man attacked a former city official, footage of the onslaught became a rallying cry. Then came another video, and another—and the story turned inside out.
author: LAUREN SMILEY

title: The ‘Lonely Runner’ Problem Only Appears Simple | WIRED
description: Take a group of runners circling a track at unique, constant paces. Answering the question of how many will always end up running alone, no matter their speed, has vexed mathematicians for decades.
author: PAULINA ROWIŃSKA

title: Best Kitchen Composters and Food Recyclers (2026) | WIRED
description: Responsibly dispose of your food scraps with one of these indoor, (mostly) odor-free, WIRED-tested devices.
author: KAT MERCK

title: Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In | WIRED
description: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.
author: BOONE ASHWORTH

title: Marine Animals in the Strait of Hormuz Don’t Get a Ceasefire | WIRED
description: As ships return to the Strait of Hormuz, mines, sonar, and congestion continue to reshape the Gulf beneath the surface.
author: EVANGELINE ELSA

title: MAGA Is Increasingly Convinced the Trump Assassination Attempt Was Staged | WIRED
description: Conspiracy theories about the Butler, Pennsylvania, shooting have ramped up in recent weeks as once steadfast Trump supporters turn on the president.
author: DAVID GILBERT

title: We Love the Bose QuietComfort Ultra 2, Especially at $50 Off | WIRED
description: The Bose QuietComfort Ultra 2 have the best active noise cancellation on the market, and they very rarely get cheaper.
author: LOURYN STRAMPE

title: ‘Beer, Titties, Freedom’: Inside the World’s Toughest Off-Road Race | WIRED
description: Every winter, 80,000 people gather in the California desert for King of the Hammers, a popular racing series featuring high-speed relays and rock crawling with dirt bikes, stock VW Bugs, and million-dollar trophy trucks.
author: MATTHEW REAMER

title: Hyundai Ioniq 3 2026: Price, Specs, Availability | WIRED
description: The brand’s new 300-mile urban EV has great interior space and driving smarts, but a lot of competition. And, yes, there's a chance it is US-bound.
author: JEREMY WHITE

title: "Uncanny Valley": OpenAI and Musk Fight Again; DOJ Mishandles Voter Data; Artemis II Comes Home | WIRED
description: In this episode, the hosts discuss the fight between OpenAI and Elon Musk, the misuse of voter data, and Artemis II’s moonshot.
author: BRIAN BARRETT

title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce? | WIRED
description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.
author: BOONE ASHWORTH

title: Telegram Is Still Hosting a Sanctioned $21 Billion Crypto Scammer Black Market | WIRED
description: The UK designated Xinbi Guarantee as an enabler of crypto scammers and human trafficking weeks ago. Telegram is still hosting it in plain sight.
author: ANDY GREENBERG

title: 6,000 Meters Under the Pacific, Japan Seeks Independence From China on Rare Earths | WIRED
description: Tokyo is succeeding where the rest of the world has failed, reducing its reliance on Beijing for crucial rare earth elements—thanks to an enormous underwater deposit discovered on a remote island.
author: LORENZO LAMPERTI

title: The Shocking Secrets of Madison Square Garden’s Surveillance Machine | WIRED
description: Famously vengeful Knicks owner Jim Dolan has long spied on people at his iconic arenas. WIRED goes deep inside the operation that allegedly tracked a trans woman, lawyers, protesters, and more.
author: NOAH SHACHTMAN

title: He Started a Social Network Alone. Then 5 Million People Signed Up | WIRED
description: Issam Hijazi launched UpScrolled after users alleged censorship on other platforms. Nine months later, its user base is soaring—while Hijazi tries to catch up with his own success.
author: KATIE DRUMMOND

title: Musk v. Altman Is a Battle for OpenAI’s Soul | WIRED
description: In Musk v. Altman, a jury will soon determine whether OpenAI has strayed from its founding mission to ensure AGI benefits humanity. Here’s what to know.
author: MAXWELL ZEFF

title: How Can Astronauts Tell How Fast They’re Going? | WIRED
description: Weirdly, spaceships have no direct way to gauge their own speed. Luckily, we can use some physics tricks to figure it out.
author: RHETT ALLAIN

title: Geothermal Everywhere: Finding the Energy to Save the World | WIRED
description: Jamie Beard is pouring everything into a singular vision: Tap into the awesome potential of geothermal power in Texas, and beyond. She has no time to lose.
author: MARIA STRESHINSKY

title: The 24 Best Movies on Amazon Prime, WIRED's Picks (February 2025) | WIRED
description: “Sinners,” “Wicked,” and “Die Hard” are just a few of the movies you should be watching on Amazon Prime Video this week.
author: MATT KAMEN

title: The 10 Best TV Shows to Stream This Month (April 2026) | WIRED
description: ‘The Boys,’ ‘The Testaments,’ and ‘Stranger Things: Tales From ’85’ are just a few of the TV shows we’re bingeing this month.
author: JENNIFER M. WOOD

title: AI Drafting My Stories? Over My Dead Body | WIRED
description: AI-assisted writing is creeping into newsrooms under the guise of efficiency. But the trade-off may be more profound than publishers are willing to admit.
author: STEVEN LEVY

title: Spoofed Tankers Are Flooding the Strait of Hormuz. These Analysts Are Tracking Them | WIRED
description: Marine insurers and oil traders want to know what’s going on in one of the world’s most critical waterways. As the volume of disappearing ships in the area increases, analysts are getting creative.
author: AARIAN MARSHALL

title: ‘Avatar: Aang, The Last Airbender’ Leaked Online. Some Fans Say Paramount Deserves the Fallout | WIRED
description: After the full movie leaked, animators mourned the chance to release their work as intended. Others feel the leak is justified in light of Paramount’s marketing blunders and association with Trump.
author: MILES KLEE

title: The Weird, Twisting Tale of How China Spied on Alysa Liu and Her Dad | WIRED
description: Years before the figure skater became an Olympic superstar, a Chinese operative tried to stalk her father and monitored other US residents deemed dissidents against China. And that’s just the beginning.
author: TIMOTHY MCLAUGHLIN

title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors | WIRED
description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.
author: GIA CHAUDRY

title: MAGA Is Increasingly Convinced the Trump Assassination Attempt Was Staged | WIRED
description: Conspiracy theories about the Butler, Pennsylvania, shooting have ramped up in recent weeks as once steadfast Trump supporters turn on the president.
author: DAVID GILBERT

title: How the Vision Pro Rollout Inflamed Tensions at Apple | WIRED
description: Even before the headset’s release, the workforce at Apple Stores was under duress. Trying to get customers interested in the Vision Pro made it worse.
author: NOAM SCHEIBER

title: The Best Movies to Stream This Month (April 2026) | WIRED
description: ’28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,’ ‘Crime 101,’ and ‘Deathstalker’ are among the films deserving of your eyeballs this month
author: MATT KAMEN

title: Bassel Khartabil: The Syrian Who Fought to the Death for a Free Internet | WIRED
description: Basel Khartabil hoped the internet would lead to a flowering of freedom and openness in Syria. Then he was arrested and imprisoned by the Assad regime.
author: ALICE SU

title: Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In | WIRED
description: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.
author: BOONE ASHWORTH

title: The Gamblers Behind One of the Weirdest Cheating Mysteries in Chess Have Been Unmasked | WIRED
description: In July 1993, a disguised player entered the World Open chess tournament in Philadelphia using the name of a mathematician who died in 1957. His real identity remained unknown—until now.
author: KIT CHELLEL

title: In the Wake of Anthropic’s Mythos, OpenAI Has a New Cybersecurity Model—and Strategy | WIRED
description: OpenAI says its safeguards “sufficiently reduce cyber risk” for now, while GPT-5.4-Cyber is a new cybersecurity-focused model.
author: LILY HAY NEWMAN

title: The Pro-Iran Meme Machine Trolling Trump With AI Lego Cartoons | WIRED
description: Since the beginning of the Iran war, the group Explosive Media has released over a dozen viral videos mocking Trump and the US.
author: DAVID GILBERT

title: Our Favorite Apple Watch Has Never Been Less Expensive | WIRED
description: Our top Apple Watch recommendation fluctuates in price. If you're in the market, now's a good time to buy.
author: LOURYN STRAMPE

title: Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once | WIRED
description: Mark Zuckerberg and Jack Dorsey have different visions for how to use AI for management purposes, but both imagine a system of heightened control.
author: MILES KLEE

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down | WIRED
description: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.
author: LAUREN GOODE

title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce? | WIRED
description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.
author: BOONE ASHWORTH

title: War Memes Are Turning Conflict Into Content | WIRED
description: The jokes were funny. The systems behind them—and the reasons we keep passing around war memes as entertainment—are more serious.
author: RAND AL-HADETHI

title: The Best Movies to Stream This Month (April 2026) | WIRED
description: ’28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,’ ‘Crime 101,’ and ‘Deathstalker’ are among the films deserving of your eyeballs this month
author: MATT KAMEN

title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China | WIRED
description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.
author: JAVIER CARBAJAL

title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App | WIRED
description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.
author: DELL CAMERON

title: Where the DOGE Operatives Are Now | WIRED
description: WIRED tracked down some of the most prominent figures of last year’s DOGE invasion. Here's where they are now—in government and beyond.
author: VITTORIA ELLIOTT

title: Opposing ICE Might Save the Country. It Could Also Ruin Your Life | WIRED
description: For months, lone vibe coder Rafael Concepcion has obsessively built tools to counter the federal immigration crackdown—pivoting as he’s been outmatched. He’s also lost his job and become a target.
author: BRENDAN I. KOERNER

title: The Iran War Is Tearing MAGA Influencers Apart | WIRED
description: “The online right wasn’t supportive and there wasn’t anything that was going to change that,” says one person familiar with the Republican influencer pipeline.
author: MAKENA KELLY

title: Best Meta Glasses (2026): Ray-Ban, Oakley, AR | WIRED
description: Meta is unquestionably winning the face-wearable war. Can you trust the company? Maybe not. But these are some of the nicest glasses I've ever worn.
author: ADRIENNE SO

title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder | WIRED
description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?
author: MAXWELL ZEFF

title: Ben McKenzie Says Crypto Has a Secret Ingredient: Male Loneliness | WIRED
description: The actor-director discussed his least favorite currency and read a series of mean tweets—about us!—at our inaugural WIRED@Night event.
author: WIRED STAFF

title: The Online Fiction Boom Reimagining China’s History | WIRED
description: Chinese fantasy novels reimagine the past with modern tech and ideology. A new book argues they also help reinforce authoritarian politics.
author: ZEYI YANG

title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI | WIRED
description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.
author: MATT BURGESS

title: AI Could Democratize One of Tech's Most Valuable Resources | WIRED
description: AI is making it easier to design chips and optimize software for different silicon. Some startups envision a revolution in chipmaking.
author: WILL KNIGHT

title: Government Workers Say They’re Getting Inundated With Religion | WIRED
description: “This has never happened before,” one government employee tells WIRED. “I have never gotten a message like this from anyone.”
author: VITTORIA ELLIOTT

title: When a Houseplant Obsession Becomes a Nightmare | WIRED
description: Some of us just can’t resist the allure of the carnivorous Nepenthes. They’re beautiful, rare, and in every way life-consuming.
author: BRIAN HOWEY

title: The 11 Best Fans to Buy Before It Gets Hot Again (2026) | WIRED
description: Swap out your creaky old box fan for a new model that lights up, mists, or even follows you around the room.
author: KAT MERCK

title: Pan Am Flight 103: Robert Mueller’s 30-Year Search for Justice | WIRED
description: Known as the Lockerbie bombing, in December 1988 a bomb downed a Pan Am jet, leaving 270 dead. It was the first mass killing of Americans by terrorists. As the head of the Justice Department’s criminal division, Robert Mueller oversaw the case. And for him, it was personal.
author: GARRETT M. GRAFF

title: The Influencers Normalizing Not Having Sex | WIRED
description: From a celibate porn star to an asexual ex-Mormon, the internet is full of people who are abstaining from sex—and it’s not just incels.
author: SKYE BATTLES

title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own | WIRED
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.
author: KATIE DRUMMOND
